core_mechanism: characterizing a target outcome as a positive-Lebesgue-measure open set in a continuous parameter space of a deterministic dynamical system, giving a constructive existence proof that the solution set is not measure-zero, then distinguishing 'positive but unobservably small measure' from 'measure zero' via Monte Carlo estimation of the outcome distribution over uniformly sampled parameters
brief_reason: The abstract exhibits an admissible configuration and stroke achieving the goal and proves the set of such strokes has positive Lebesgue measure (an open event, not a measure-zero fluke), while Monte Carlo sampling of a random stroke estimates the outcome-count distribution P(k) to show the measure is nonzero yet unobservably small.
